content.problemStatement:
You are given 2 arrays **W** =  (**W1**, **W2**, .., **WN**) and **C** =  (**C1**, **C2**, .., **CN**) with **N** elements each. A range [l, r] is unique if all the elements **Cl**, **Cl+1**, .., **Cr** are unique (ie. no duplicates). The sum of the range is **Wl** +  **Wl+1** + ... + **Wr**.

You want to find an unique range with the maximum sum possible, and output this sum.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains an integer N on the first line, followed by N space-separated integers C1 to CN on the second line, and N space-separated integers W1 to WN on the third line.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximum possible sum of a contiguous sub-array whose C-values are all distinct.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The best unique sub-array is indices 1..3 (C = 1,2,0) with sum 6+7+8 = 21.
All C values are distinct, so the whole array gives sum 10+20+30 = 60.

The maximum sum comes from the sub-array indices 2..3 (C = 2,2 are not distinct, so we take 2..3 where C = 2,2? Actually distinct sub-array is just index 3 alone or 2 alone. The best is indices 2..3 after moving left, giving sum 30+40 = 70.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using a sliding window approach. We maintain a window of unique elements and keep track of the sum of the weights within this window. When we encounter a duplicate element, we shrink the window from the left until the duplicate element is removed from the window. We keep track of the maximum sum seen so far.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T*N) total. The time complexity is linear because each element is visited at most twice (once when expanding the window and once when shrinking it).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we use an array of size N to keep track of the last seen index of each element.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        C = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        W = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        last = [-1] * N
        cur = 0
        best = 0
        left = 0
        for right, val in enumerate(C):
            if last[val] >= left:
                while left <= last[val]:
                    cur -= W[left]
                    left += 1
            cur += W[right]
            if cur > best:
                best = cur
            last[val] = right
        out_lines.append(str(best))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
